Today's Decision Flow

The order of operations through your 8–12 window. Each step links to its full tab.

7:30–8:00

Pre-Open Bias Pre-Market tab

Mark the overnight map, read NY 8AM open vs the London mid. Above mid + P3 → up to 84.5% London-high break. The mid overrides the pattern.

9:00–10:00

9AM Hour Color 1H Continuation tab

Green 9AM hour → session continues up ~70%. Read it by 10:00 and lean with it.

9:30–10:30

Initial Balance Initial Balance tab

At the 10:30 close: IB above mid + low set first → 84% IB-high break by close. IB breaks either side 96% of days.

10:00–12:00

Q2 Break → PM Extreme Noon Curve tab

Q2 breaks Q1 high → PM sets the day high 82%. The day's high & low split across noon 72.8% of the time.

12:00+

Trade the PM Leg

Work the directional move toward the opposite extreme (peak ~2:04 PM — the bigger leg). Stack IB60 targets for entries.

CROC1 Playbook · NQ · Pre-Market

Pre-Market Map & Bias

What's set before NY opens — and the one question at 8:00: where is NY open vs the London mid?
The overnight build: Asia (20:00–02:00) sets the reference range, London (02:00–08:00) defines the ALN pattern, and at the 08:00 open the whole overnight map is locked. Your pre-open job is to mark the overnight levels and read one directional filter — NY 8AM open vs the London midpoint — which sets the day's lean before you take a single trade. (Pattern probabilities live in Session Patterns; this tab is the pre-open read.)
🎯 The Midpoint Filter — Your Pre-Open Directional Read
London Midpoint = (London High + London Low) / 2. NY 8AM open above the mid → London-high break rises to 84–87% across all four patterns. Below the mid → London-low break rises to 81–85%. The filter is symmetrical and so strong it overrides the pattern direction. 4,448-day midpoint study
🟢 Highest-conviction LONG: P3 + NY opens above London mid → 84.5% London-high break (n=1,165, ~70% of P3 days). Appears on ~26% of all trading days — your primary long-bias day.
🔴 Highest-conviction SHORT: P4 + NY opens below London mid → 82.2% London-low break (n=810, ~64% of P4 days). ~18% of all days — your primary short-bias day.
Overnight Levels — Hit Rate & Timing in the NY Session

Which overnight levels NY price actually touches, and when. Mark these before 8:00.

LevelHit RateMean HitMedian Hit25th75thSignal
7–8 AM Midpoint92.1%08:2708:1508:0208:32NEAR CERTAIN
Asia High78.8%08:4708:0508:0008:48HIGH
2–3 AM Midpoint74.3%09:0308:3508:2509:05HIGH
London High73.9%09:1308:3608:2809:18HIGH
Asia Low71.6%08:5108:2908:0008:56MODERATE
Midnight Open69.4%09:1008:3708:2809:16MODERATE
London Low67.8%09:1208:3908:3009:18MODERATE
Timing: the 7–8AM midpoint is a near-certain early magnet (92%, median touch 8:15). Every level's 75th-percentile touch is inside the first ~1.5 hours — your 8AM–12PM window captures the large majority of level interaction.
RTH Open vs Prior-Day RTH Range
Opens OUTSIDE prior RTH (gap day): 83.3% chance price does NOT break the opposite side of prior RTH. Use the opposite side as hard S/R — fading toward it is favored.
Opens INSIDE prior RTH (inside day): 72.7% chance at least one side breaks during RTH. Expect expansion — bias toward whichever side the open is nearest.
Pre-Open Bias Matrix — Pattern × NY 8AM vs London Mid

Sorted by London-high break %. The midpoint position overrides the pattern. 4,448-day study

PatternNY PositionnLH BreakLL BreakConviction
P2 — Asia EngulfsAbove Mid7087.1%54.3%EXTREME
P4 — Partial DownAbove Mid45485.0%60.4%HIGHEST
P3 — Partial UpAbove Mid1,16584.5%55.2%HIGHEST
P1 — London EngulfsAbove Mid71084.1%53.0%HIGHEST
P2 — Asia EngulfsBelow Mid10068.0%85.0%EXTREME
P1 — London EngulfsBelow Mid63556.4%83.0%HIGHEST
P4 — Partial DownBelow Mid81054.7%82.2%HIGHEST
P3 — Partial UpBelow Mid49468.6%81.2%HIGH
Key takeaway: P4 (bearish pattern) opening above the mid still yields 85.0% London-high break; P3 (bullish) opening below yields 81.2% London-low break. Check the midpoint first — every time.
Pre-Open Checklist
7:30

Mark the overnight map

Plot Asia H/L, London H/L, the 2AM & 7AM mids, midnight open, and PDH/PDL. Identify the ALN pattern (see Session Patterns) and compute the London midpoint.

7:58

Light the candle · set the read

Note where the 8:00 open sits vs the London mid — this is your directional lean. Above mid = long bias; below = short bias. The mid overrides the pattern.

8:00

Confirm conviction Go / no-go

Pattern + midpoint agree (e.g. P3 above mid, or P4 below mid) → highest-conviction day, size up. Pattern and mid disagree → trust the mid, size down. Gap vs inside day tells you whether to fade the opposite extreme or expect expansion.

TARGETS

Queue the magnets

The 7–8AM mid is a 92% early magnet (median 8:15) — often the first target. Then the London level in your bias direction (74% hit). Stack with IB and Noon Curve targets once those windows form.

Nasdaq-100 E-mini Futures · NQ · 10-Year Study · 2016–2026

ALN Sessions

Overnight Range Structural Analysis · Asia / London / New York · n = 2,542 sessions
ALN Sessions is a structural framework that examines how the Asia session (20:00–02:00) and London session (02:00–08:00) interact overnight to form four distinct range patterns, each with measurable implications for the New York session (08:00–16:00). Patterns are classified at the 8:00 bar close — the final London bar — and probabilities reflect how often New York price action breaks the London high, London low, or both.
Asia 20:00 – 02:00
London 02:00 – 08:00
New York 08:00 – 16:00
Pattern classified at 08:00 bar close
Session Structure
ASIA SESSION
20:00 → 02:00
Sets overnight reference range
LONDON SESSION
02:00 → 08:00
Defines pattern vs Asia · Pattern locked at 08:00
NEW YORK SESSION
08:00 → 16:00
Measured outcome window
Total Sessions
2,542
Mar 2016 – Mar 2026
Highest Confidence Pattern
P3
High prob of high break
Lowest Confidence Pattern
P1
Coin-flip first-break odds
Breaks Neither Side
<1%
Across all 4 patterns

Pattern Structure — Session Range Diagrams
Pattern 1 — London Engulfs Asia
P1
London high > Asia high · London low < Asia low
Pattern 2 — Asia Engulfs London
P2
Asia high > London high · Asia low < London low
Pattern 3 — Partial Engulf Up
P3
London high > Asia high · London low inside Asia range
Pattern 4 — Partial Engulf Down
P4
London low < Asia low · London high inside Asia range
Asia session range London session range Asia H/L reference
How To Use It Intraday
08:00 — read the pattern at the London close. It sets the lean. P3 → lean long (80.8% London-high break). P4 → lean short (75.0% London-low break). P1 → no lean, trade the first break. P2 → expect a directional break out of the coil — highest break rates of any pattern, but rare.
Let the first NY break confirm — or kill — the edge. P3 with the low breaking first, or P4 with the high breaking first, drops the edge ~29pp to a coin flip (51.2% / 46.2%) — stand down rather than chase. With P2, a low-first break actually raises the high-break odds to 59.8% (coil whipsaw).
Stack with the London-mid filter. Cross-check against NY 8AM open vs the London mid (Pre-Market tab). When the ALN lean and the mid filter agree, that's the size-up day; when they disagree, size down.
Nasdaq-100 E-mini Futures · NQ · 10-Year Study · 2016–2026

Initial Balance Breaks

Initial Balance Range Analysis · 09:30 – 10:30 Eastern · n = 2,571 sessions
The Initial Balance (IB) is defined as the 09:30 – 10:30 ET range (the first hour of the NY equity session). This study examines sessions where the first post-IB bar opens inside the IB range, then tracks how often price breaches the IB high or low by noon (12:00 ET) and by the session close (16:00 ET). A breach is defined as any bar wick trading beyond an IB level by at least 1 tick (0.25 pts). Two confluences are tested for directional edge: where the IB closed relative to its own midpoint, and the order in which the IB high and low were established during the IB window.
Qualifying Days 2,571
IB Window 09:30 – 10:30 ET
Breach Any wick > 1 tick beyond level
Filter Next bar opens inside IB range
Initial Balance — Structural Diagram
Initial Balance — Key Levels & Concepts
ILLUSTRATIVE EXAMPLE

Midpoint Confluence

Where did the IB close relative to its own midpoint? A close above the midpoint is associated with higher IB high breach rates; a close below is associated with higher IB low breach rates.

▲ IB CLOSE ABOVE MIDPOINT
n = 1,405 days · 54.6% of qualifying days
IB High Break · Noon70.1%
IB High Break · Close82.3%
▼ IB CLOSE BELOW MIDPOINT
n = 1,156 days · 44.9% of qualifying days
IB Low Break · Noon65.8%
IB Low Break · Close76.5%
IB High / Low Order Confluence

Which IB extreme was printed last? The final swing of the IB window reflects late directional momentum. 4 days excluded where the opening bar simultaneously established both the IB high and low.

▲ LOW SET FIRST · HIGH SET LAST
n = 1,298 days · 50.5% of classified days
IB High Break · Noon68.2%
IB High Break · Close80.9%
▼ HIGH SET FIRST · LOW SET LAST
n = 1,269 days · 49.3% of classified days
IB Low Break · Noon57.8%
IB Low Break · Close71.2%
Combined Confluence · Highest Probability Setups

Both confluences aligned in the same direction — IB midpoint close and HL order pointing the same way.

▲ CLOSE ABOVE MID + LOW FIRST
n = 1,114 days · IB closed above midpoint AND IB high set last
IB High Break · Noon74.0%
IB High Break · Close84.0%
▼ CLOSE BELOW MID + HIGH FIRST
n = 974 days · IB closed below midpoint AND IB low set last
IB Low Break · Noon67.9%
IB Low Break · Close78.0%
Key Takeaways
IB Almost Always Breaks

96.1% of qualifying days see at least one side breached by the 4pm close. Trading entirely inside a closed IB through the full session is statistically very rare.

High Breaks Dominate

IB High breaches outpace IB Low breaches by ~8pp at both timeframes, consistent with NQ's long-term structural uptrend across the 10-year study window.

Strongest Setup (Bullish)

Close above mid + Low first → 74.0% by noon / 84.0% by close for a high break. Combined, both confluences add ~27pp over the unfiltered high-break baseline.

Vol% TiernMult60RTH MedClose inside ±1×

Critical: Low vol% days have a pronounced downside bias — 66% break below vs. 45% break above. These are continuation-sell or gap-down sessions.

10:30–11:59 ET

14:00+ ET

Hard Rule: Stop trading IB expansion setups at noon ET. 68% of the edge window closes before noon. Late breaks (14:00+) are exclusively one-directional and the day's range is mostly consumed.

IB60 (pts)Predicted RTHMult60T1 Beyond ExtremeT2 Beyond Extreme

Regression: RTH ≈ (IB60 × 1.48) + 69 pts  |  T1 = extreme ± (IB60 × 0.65)  |  T2 = extreme ± (IB60 × 1.22)
Nasdaq-100 E-mini Futures · NQ · 2020–2026 touch study + 17-yr directional study

Midpoints

Overnight & pre-open midpoints as magnets and directional filters
Concept: a session midpoint = (High + Low) / 2 of its window. Midpoints do two jobs — they're magnets (price returns to touch them at 90%+ rates) and directional filters (which side of the mid the 9:30 open lands on predicts where the day closes). Below: the 2AM London & 7AM pre-open mids as touch targets, then a 17-year study of all three windows as a directional read.
🎯 Two Jobs — Near-Certain Magnet, and a Same-Side Directional Edge

The Paradox — Touch Probability Increases with Distance
2AM Mid — Touch Rate by Excursion Distance
Excursion from MidTouch RateImplication
0–10 pts86%Price barely moved
10–20 pts93%Strong pull
20–30 pts94%Still very high
50–75 pts99%Exhaustion signal
100+ pts100%All returned
7AM Mid — Touch Rate by Excursion Distance
Excursion from MidTouch RateImplication
0–10 pts81%Price barely moved
10–20 pts87%Standard retrace
30–40 pts92%Still magnetic
80–100 pts100%Every day returned
150–200 pts100%Extreme mean rev.
The Paradox Rule: NEVER abandon a midpoint level because price moved far away. The further price moves from either mid, the higher the return probability. Extended moves represent exhaustion, not continuation. The only time to abandon: a strong one-directional London move with no retrace attempt for 2+ hours (the 6–10% of "trend days").
Confluence Setup — When Both Mids Are Active
ScenarioFrequencyAction
2AM mid untouched at 7AM ~15% of sessions Both 2AM AND 7AM mids become active targets. Strongest setup of the day.
2AM mid = 7AM mid (within 10 pts) Occasional EXTREME CONFLUENCE — two independent 90%+ probability levels stacked at same price. Day's primary target.
2AM mid touched, 7AM mid not ~75% of sessions Standard setup — only 7AM mid is active during your session. Use as primary intraday reference.
Both mids touched before 8AM Rare Both levels resolved. Look for next structural levels (session highs/lows, pRTH).
New Study · 17-Year Directional Edge
The 9:30 Open vs Midpoint Predicts Day Direction
4,400 NQ sessions · Dec 2008–Mar 2026 · Three midpoints tested + confluence trade
What's new: The 2AM and 7AM studies above measure touch probability. This new study measures directional edge — which side of each midpoint the 9:30 cash open lands on, and how strongly that predicts where the day closes. Three windows tested across 17 years (vs 6 years above): adds the wide London 2–8 AM full window, and quantifies the confluence trade when all three align.

Role: Magnet target
Directional Prediction from 9:30 — Mode B (Cash Open Onward)
For each midpoint, the probability of closing on the same side as 9:30, versus the unconditional baseline. Edge = conditional probability − baseline probability in percentage points. The 9:30 cash open beats the 8:00 futures open as a directional read every time.
Midpoint n P(close above | 9:30 above) Edge above P(close below | 9:30 below) Edge below Baseline
London 2–8 AM 4,421 64.4% +9.0 pp 55.3% +10.7 pp 55.4%
Asian 2–3 AM STRONGEST 4,401 69.0% +13.6 pp 59.7% +15.1 pp 55.4%
Pre-open 7–8 AM 4,421 62.9% +8.7 pp 54.8% +9.0 pp 54.2%
The take: Hit rate decreases as the window moves earlier in the night; directional edge moves the opposite direction. The 2–3 AM midpoint is the highest-information bias filter available. The 7–8 AM midpoint is the highest-probability magnet target. The 2–8 AM London midpoint is both, moderately.
9:30 Confluence — When All Three Midpoints Align
The headline finding: When 9:30 prints above all three midpoints (36.5% of all days), the day closes above the London mid 66.9% of the time — a +11.5 pp edge that exceeds any single midpoint signal. When 9:30 is below all three (32.4% of days), the close lands below 59.0% — a +14.4 pp edge. Together this covers 69% of all sessions. Mixed states carry zero edge.
The Monotonic Edge Ladder
9:30 State n % of Sample P(close above London mid) Edge vs Baseline Conviction
3-above — above all three mids 1,606 36.5% 66.9% +11.5 pp LONG BIAS
2-above — above two of three 761 17.3% 60.3% +4.9 pp WEAK
1-above — mixed signals 607 13.8% 52.9% −2.6 pp NO EDGE
0-above — below all three mids 1,426 32.4% 41.0% +14.4 pp SHORT BIAS
Baseline (all sessions) 4,400 100% 55.5% — —
Clean monotonic step from −14.4 pp (all below) up to +11.5 pp (all above). The signal lives in the alignment — mixed states (1-of-3, 2-of-3) carry weak or no edge. Trade direction only when full alignment exists.
Joint Edge — When Confluence Predicts Closing Confluence
3-above → close above ALL three
61.9%
Baseline 49.0% · Edge +12.9 pp
0-above → close below ALL three
54.6%
Baseline 39.3% · Edge +15.3 pp
Recent Regime · May 2024 – May 2026 · The Edge Has Strengthened

40.4%

68.3%
Edge +14.8 pp

63.2%
Edge +16.7 pp
Median pre-hit excursion
~75 pts
75th pct ~130 pts
Vol regime warning: 24mo median pre-hit excursion is ~75 pts vs ~18 pts all-time. Stop sizing must scale ~4× — a stop tighter than ~65 pts is at meaningful risk of being taken out on a typical successful midpoint pull. Right-size or skip the trade.
17-Year Stability — Confluence Edge by Year (3-above case)
Edge (in pp) of "9:30 above all three mids → close above London mid" vs the year's baseline. Positive every single year for 18 years; only one weak reading (2021 at +1.4 pp).
Year 3-above n % of year P(close↑ Lon) Baseline Edge

Operational Rules — Which Midpoint for Which Job
01

Use Asian 2–3 AM mid as the directional filter

Strongest read in the study (+13.6 / +15.1 pp; +15.3 / +18.1 pp in last 24mo). If 9:30 prints above the 2–3 AM mid, the day closes above it 69% of the time. Use this as the binary "long bias / allow shorts" gate for the session.

02

Use 7–8 AM mid as the magnet target

80.9% hit rate Mode B, 94.2% Mode A. Use this as the primary objective for fade-to-mid entries from the 9:30 cash open — most touches occur within 30 minutes. Combines with the existing 90.1% touch-by-9:30 finding above.

03

Use 2–8 AM London mid as context

Middle-ground hit rate and edge. Use as an interim resistance/support reference between the 2–3 AM bias level and the 7–8 AM target. A 9:30 print far from London mid but close to 7–8 AM mid (or vice versa) is information about which side dominates.

04

Confluence trade is the highest-conviction trade

When 9:30 prints above (or below) all three midpoints simultaneously — 69% of all sessions — directional edge climbs to +11.5 / +14.4 pp all-time, +14.8 / +16.7 pp in last 24mo. Mixed states (1-of-3, 2-of-3) carry near-zero edge. Wait for full alignment before trading direction off this filter.

05

Skip if 9:30 is 80+ pts from your target mid

For Asian 2–3 AM: hit rate falls to 48.1% at this distance — the only sub-50% cell in the entire study. For pre-open 7–8 AM: 58.6%. These are gap or trend-day signatures — alignment with the 130 SMA matters more than reversion. Don't fade into extreme distance.

06

Size stops to current vol — ~75 pts not ~20 pts

Median pre-hit excursion in last 24mo is ~75 pts across the windows. All-time median was ~18 pts. A stop tighter than ~65 pts is at meaningful risk of being taken out on a typical successful day in current vol. Right-size or skip the play.

07

Use post-hit asymmetry on the second leg

Across all three midpoints: from below, hit-then-continue-through wins ~55%. From above, hit-then-revert-back wins ~54%. Net bullish skew supports letting long fades run further than short fades — consistent with CROC1's long-only default plus one discretionary short.

Methodology: NQ 1-min OHLCV, Dec 2008–Mar 2026, n≈4,400 sessions. Each midpoint = (High + Low) / 2 across its window. "Mode B" = NY session defined as 9:30 AM – 4:59 PM ET (cash open onward). "Hit" = any NY 1-min bar whose [low, high] contains the midpoint. Edge = P(matching close direction | matching open side) − unconditional P. Source timestamps Central; shifted +1h to ET. Days require ≥60 London bars (≥20 for single-hour windows), ≥150 NY bars, non-zero range.
Nasdaq-100 E-mini Futures · NQ · 10-Year Study · 2016–2026

Noon Curve

Time-Based Range Analysis · 08:00 – 16:00 Eastern · n = 2,479 sessions
The Noon Curve is a structural framework applied to a custom Time-Based Range (TBR) spanning 8:00am to 4:00pm Eastern on the NQ futures contract. The range is divided at noon — the Noon Curve midpoint — creating an AM window (8:00–12:00) and a PM window (12:00–16:00). The core thesis: in most sessions, the TBR high forms on one side of the midpoint and the TBR low forms on the opposite side. It is statistically rare for both the high and the low to be contained entirely within the AM or entirely within the PM. The three anchor points — 8:00am open, 12:00pm midpoint, and 4:00pm close — define the geometry of the session.
08:00 TBR Open · Noon Curve anchor
12:00 Noon Curve midpoint · AM / PM divide
16:00 TBR Close · Noon Curve anchor

Noon Curve Quarterly Confluence
Quarter 2 break confluence
Within the AM session, Quarter 1 (8:00–10:00) establishes the initial range — a high and a low. When Quarter 2 (10:00–12:00) subsequently breaks one side of that Q1 range, it provides directional confluence for how the full session is likely to resolve.
When Q2 breaks the Q1 high, the data shows the AM session goes on to set the TBR low and the PM session sets the TBR high in 82.12% of opposite-side sessions — suggesting the Q2 upside break exhausts AM buying, leaving the PM to extend to new highs while the AM anchors the session low.
Conversely, when Q2 breaks the Q1 low, the AM sets the TBR high and the PM sets the TBR low in 72.42% of opposite-side sessions. When Q2 breaks both sides or neither, the signal collapses to a near coin-flip with no meaningful edge.
Example: Q2 breaks Q1 high
STRUCTURAL EXAMPLE
TBR low forms in Q1 before the break · PM sets TBR high · 82.12% outcome
TBR low sets in Q1 (before break) Q2 breaks Q1 high TBR high sets in PM

Nasdaq-100 E-mini Futures · NQ · 10-Year Study · 2016–2026

1H Continuation

Opening Hour Bias Analysis · CME Globex 18:00 – 17:00 Eastern · n = 2,472 sessions
The 1H Continuation concept examines whether the direction of a key opening hour on the NQ futures contract predicts the directional outcome of the broader session. Two signal hours are studied: the 6PM Hour (18:00–19:00 ET), which captures the initial Globex open reaction, and the 9AM Hour (09:00–10:00 ET), which captures the NY equity market open and the surrounding pre-market activity. For each signal, two outcome windows are measured — the Full Session (17:00 close vs 18:00 open) and the NY Equity Session (16:00 close vs 09:30 open). A continuation is recorded when the outcome window closes in the same direction as the signal hour. 1 tick = 0.25 points.
6PM Hour 19:00 close vs 18:00 open
9AM Hour 10:00 close vs 09:00 open
Full Session 17:00 close vs 18:00 open
NY Session 16:00 close vs 09:30 open

All Scenarios · Signal Hour × Outcome Window
How To Trade It
The 9AM hour is the real signal — read the candle color at 10:00 and lean with it. Green 9AM → NY session continues up 70.6% and the full session 69.2%. Red 9AM → NY continues down 62.8%. Both are tradeable edges that land right as the IB window opens.
Stack it with the London-mid bias and the IB tell. 9AM color agreeing with the 8AM open-vs-London-mid read and the IB midpoint close is the full-size condition; any conflict between the three reads means half size or stand down.
The 6PM Globex hour is informational only — never an entry trigger. Green 6PM carries a moderate 60.2% full-session edge at best; a red 6PM is a coin flip (49.3%) and actually leans reversal on the NY window (45.0% continuation). Note it in the overnight frame, then wait for the 9AM print.
Nasdaq-100 E-mini Futures · NQ · 4,309 RTH days · 2008–2026

ATR Odds Engine

Expected-move hit probabilities · directional + time-decayed · noon-stop tuned
NQ · 4,309 RTH days · 2008–2026 · 8AM–12PM ET touches
Concept: the other tabs tell you which direction; the ATR engine tells you how far is reachable, and by when. It takes today's volatility (ATR) and your anchor, lays expected-move bands above and below, and scores each band's probability of being touched in your 8AM–12PM window — adjusted for direction and for how much of the session is already gone.
How the Odds Engine Works
Two inputs do the work: a directional base rate (how often a band that far out gets touched at all, from 4,309 RTH days) × a time-decay factor (odds bleed down as the morning passes — a level untouched by 10:30 rarely fires before noon).
Bands are ATR-scaled, IB-anchored: RTH range ≈ IB60 × 1.48 + 69 pts; the IB60 multiplier runs ~2.0×. Practical targets land near T1 = ±IB60 × 0.65 and T2 = ±IB60 × 1.22 — the engine places these for you off the live ATR.
Read it directionally: set the bias (auto = price vs London mid). Bands with the bias get the full base rate; bands against it are discounted. A band pinned to the midpoint is chop — skip it.
Inputs
Level Odds — Probability of Touch Before Noon
Continuation Ladder

Read

Lower bands are fadeable scalps — bigger bounce, but the durable hold is post-noon. A band pinned to the midpoint is chop — skip it. Odds bleed down through the morning; a level untouched by 10:30 rarely fires before noon.

How To Use It Intraday
8:00

Fill from alerts & set the bias

Hit "Fill from alerts" to pull mid/current and compute the anchor + ATR(21). Set bias to Auto (price vs London mid) so the ladder discounts against-trend bands automatically.

PICK

Trade the highest-odds band in your direction T1 / T2

T1 (~IB60 × 0.65) is the high-probability first target; T2 (~IB60 × 1.22) is the stretch. Take the band with the strongest touch odds that sits with your pre-open bias — not against it.

DECAY

Respect the clock

Odds fall through the morning. A band untouched by 10:30 rarely fires before noon — don't hold a stale target into your noon stop. Lower bands are fadeable scalps; the durable hold is post-noon.

Odds = directional base rate × time-decay factor (NQ RTH-anchored). Bounce = median favorable reaction within 30 min off an isolated band. Empirical, not predictive — sizing and stops are yours.

💰 Account Baseline
Set a baseline date when starting a new funded account. Balance shown in the header counts only trades closed on or after this date. Trade history and stats are preserved.
⚡ Finnhub — Market News API
Free at finnhub.io — no country restrictions, 60 calls/min, real financial news. Key available instantly after signup.
Falls back to Claude web search automatically if no key set.
📡 ntfy.sh — Level Auto-Fetch
Zapier reads your TradingView alert email → posts to ntfy.sh → CROC1 fetches and fills all levels automatically. Set up your Zapier zap first, then enter your channel name below.
If Auto-Fetch fails, use 📋 Paste & Fill — copy the ntfy message then click the button in Morning Brief.

NYOPEN: {{close}}
1-min chart · bar open · updates NY Open field + shows LONG/SHORT bias vs London Mid

IBFORMING HOD: {{high}} LOD: {{low}}
1-min chart · bar close at 10:00 ET · tracks IB range building

1-min chart · bar close at 10:30 ET · IB is set, confirms extension targets
Alert 5 — 12:00 PM ET · Noon Curve

1-min chart · bar close at 12:00 ET · triggers noon stop rule, captures AM extreme
